nuggets.headline: Nigel Godrich's 'deep and profound' bond with Radiohead nearly took a detour for 'In Rainbows'
nuggets.text: The creative partnership between Radiohead and producer Nigel Godrich is legendary, with Godrich producing every one of their albums since *OK Computer*. For *In Rainbows*, however, the band initially sought a different path, working with producer Mark Stent in early sessions. Bassist Colin Greenwood explained that they felt their work with Godrich had become "a little too safe," but ultimately, they returned to Godrich to complete the album, highlighting the indispensable nature of their collaboration. Godrich himself has described his relationship with Radiohead as one he could only have with one band, calling it "very deep and profound".

source.title: In Rainbows - Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
source.quoteSnippet: In the February 2006 sessions, they chose to work with producer Mark Stent instead of their longtime co-producer Nigel Godrich. Bassist Colin Greenwood, commenting on their decision, said 'Nigel and the band know each other so well now, it's all got a little too safe.' ... I can only ever have one band like Radiohead who I've worked with for this many years. That's a very deep and profound relationship.

nuggets.imageCaption: Nigel Godrich (left) in his other band, Ultraísta, alongside Laura Bettinson.

nuggets.headline: 'Weird Fishes/Arpeggi' emerged from a three-year recording process that almost broke Radiohead
nuggets.text: The creation of *In Rainbows*, featuring this track, was a grueling three-year journey that pushed Radiohead to their limits. The band members had taken a hiatus to have children, and when they reconvened, they described the studio atmosphere as "dead." Thom Yorke stated the recording process "pretty much half killed us," and that whether the band would continue was "in the balance." It was only towards the end of this intense period that they felt they had truly made the record they wanted, with "Weird Fishes/Arpeggi" being one of the songs mixed in the final stages of this challenging production.

source.title: “It pretty much half killed us. Whether the band would continue was in the balance”: The Radiohead album that almost broke up the band, turned the music industry on its head - and became their best record
source.quoteSnippet: The recording of the album took three years, which is a long time by anybody’s standards. It pretty much half killed us. We’d all stopped to have kids. When we got back into the studio, it was just dead. It was only at that point that we completely believed that we'd made the record that we wanted to.

nuggets.imageCaption: Thom Yorke performing live in 2006, during the In Rainbows recording period.

nuggets.headline: Thom Yorke and Nigel Godrich's collaboration extends to Atoms for Peace
nuggets.text: If you're drawn to the layered, intricate sound of Radiohead, you should explore Atoms for Peace, a supergroup formed by Radiohead frontman Thom Yorke and their longtime producer Nigel Godrich. The band, which also includes Red Hot Chili Peppers bassist Flea and drummer Joey Waronker (who worked with Godrich on Beck's *Mutations*), started as an attempt to translate Yorke's solo album *The Eraser* into a live setting. They swiftly evolved, releasing their debut album *AMOK* in 2013, which further blurs the lines between electronic and acoustic instrumentation.

source.title: A New Career in a New Town: Thom Yorke and Nigel Godrich run AMOK as Atoms for Peace - Fact Magazine
source.quoteSnippet: February 25 will see Atoms for Peace – a collaboration between Radiohead frontman Thom Yorke and the band’s producer Nigel Godrich – release their debut album AMOK. Enlisting musicians Flea (Red Hot Chili Peppers, The Mars Volta), Joey Waronker (drummer for Beck and later REM) and Mauro Refosco (percussionist for David Byrne and others), Atoms for Peace formed as an attempt to recreate music from Yorke’s 2006 solo album The Eraser in a live setting, but swiftly morphed into something different.

nuggets.imageCaption: Atoms for Peace, featuring Thom Yorke and Nigel Godrich.

artistSummary: Radiohead is an English alternative rock band renowned for their experimental approach and evolving sound. Formed in Oxfordshire, the band has consistently pushed musical boundaries, creating critically acclaimed albums that blend rock with electronic textures and intricate songwriting.
